message:
fix(storage): default busy timeout, coalesce optional text on import, add error propagation tests

Three functional changes and accompanying test coverage:

1. DEFAULT_BUSY_TIMEOUT_MS (30s) constant replaces the implicit
   None-means-no-timeout in SqliteStorage::open(). Previously, open()
   passed None to open_with_timeout(), meaning newly opened connections
   had no busy timeout and would fail immediately on SQLITE_BUSY instead
   of retrying. This was inconsistent with open_memory() which already
   had a hardcoded timeout. Now both paths use the same 30-second
   default, preventing spurious failures under concurrent access.

2. upsert_issue_for_import now writes empty strings instead of SQL NULL
   for the design and acceptance_criteria columns. These columns are
   logically optional at the application layer (Option<String>), but
   importing NULL values caused inconsistency with the normal create
   path which always writes text. Coalescing to empty strings ensures
   roundtrip fidelity: export -> import -> read yields the same
   semantics regardless of whether the issue was created locally or
   imported.

3. A new test in create.rs (test_create_issue_with_parent_propagates_
   storage_error) validates that the ? operator changes from the
   previous commit actually surface database errors through
   create_issue_impl when the parent exists but the DB becomes
   corrupted mid-operation (simulated by dropping the issues table).

4. A new integration test (export_import_roundtrip_keeps_optional_text_
   fields_integrity_safe) in jsonl_import_export.rs verifies the
   roundtrip behavior of optional text fields through the full
   export/import pipeline.

5. A new unit test (test_open_uses_default_busy_timeout) confirms that
   SqliteStorage::open() successfully operates under a concurrent
   write lock, proving the busy timeout is active.

Also includes minor rustfmt reformatting in config/mod.rs, main.rs,
sync/mod.rs, and sqlite.rs (line wrapping adjustments, no logic changes).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
